summary: Completed testing of Colori & Font (Design & Style) feature in Bruno Melito Hair salon app. All 15 backend tests passed and all frontend UI tests passed. The feature allows admin to customize colors (primary, accent, background, text) and fonts (display, body) for the public website.
passed_tests: TEST 1: Login with admin@brunomelito.it / Admin123! works correctly
TEST 2: Navigate to 'Gestione Sito' page via /gestione-sito URL
TEST 3: All 7 tabs present: Generale, Colori & Font, Servizi, Foto Salone, Gallery Lavori, Recensioni, Orari & Contatti
TEST 4: Colori & Font tab: color pickers for primary_color, accent_color, bg_color, text_color visible and functional
TEST 5: Colori & Font tab: font selectors for font_display, font_body visible
TEST 6: Colori & Font tab: color preview section shows 4 colored squares (Primario, Accento, Sfondo, Testo)
TEST 7: Colori & Font tab: font preview section shows sample text with correct fonts
TEST 8: Save config button works - changes a color, saves, shows toast 'Configurazione salvata!'
TEST 9: Color changes persist after page reload
TEST 10: Public website (/) loads and displays correctly with colors from config
TEST 11: /api/public/website returns config with primary_color, accent_color, bg_color, text_color, font_display, font_body
TEST 13: PUT /api/website/config persists color changes to database
TEST 14: PUT /api/website/config persists font changes to database
critical_code_review_comments: NOTE: The sidebar navigation link shows '/gestione-sito' but the review_request mentioned '/sito-admin' - the correct URL is /gestione-sito
seed_data_creation: None - using existing admin account and default config data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Colori & Font feature fully implemented and tested. The WebsiteAdminPage at /gestione-sito has 7 tabs including the Design tab (Colori & Font). Colors and fonts can be customized via admin panel and are correctly reflected on the public website at /. The public API /api/public/website includes all design fields in the config object. Backend uses DEFAULT_WEBSITE_CONFIG in routes/public.py (line 111) which now includes design fields.
